perturbation.original_text: 6. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the C ompany would not have an adequate remedy at law and would be irreparably damaged by any unau thorized disclosure or use of any - 4 - 31244051.4 Confidential Information or in the event that any o f the provisions of this Agreement were not performed by the Recipient and the Recipient’s Repr esentatives in accordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached by the Recipient o r any of the Recipient's Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Company would not have an adequate remedy at law and would be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Information. However, such liability will not extend to indirect or consequential damages arising from failure of the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives to perform this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that the Company would be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure. The modified text then introduces an exception by stating that liability will not extend to indirect or consequential damages in event of any of the provisions of this agreement were not performed, contradicting the absolute nature of the original statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Company would not have an adequate remedy at law and would be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure or use of any Confidential Information. However, such liability will not extend to indirect or consequential damages arising from failure of the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives to perform this Agreement Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to the Receiver, the Recipient agrees that the Receiver shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to equitable relief, including an injunct ion or specific performance, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of the provisions of th is Agreement by the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives. Such remedies shall n ot be deemed to be exclusive remedies but shall be in addition to all other remedies availabl e at law or equity to the Receiver or the Company. In the event of litigation relating to th is Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that the Recipient or any of the Recipie nt's Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the Recipient shall be liable and p ay to the Receiver the reasonable costs and expenses (including attorney's fees on a full indem nity solicitor and his own client basis) incurred by the Receiver in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. The Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Receiver and the Receiver's directors, officers, employees, consultants, representatives, advisors and agents f rom all damages and losses of any nature whatsoever (including consequential damages) arisin g out of a breach by the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives of any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Company would be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure, implying no limitations on the damages recoverable. The changed text adds an exception, stating that liability will not extend to indirect or consequential damages. This directly contradicts the original assertion of being 'irreparably damaged', as it limits the type of damages recoverable. The contradicted text includes the original statement but the changed text modifies this, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any computer files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic computer system backup. However, if the Recipient has the technological ability to encrypt and completely erase them at a reasonable cost, they must do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Recipient is 'not required to destroy' backup files under specific conditions. The added sentence imposes a *conditional requirement* to destroy them if encryption and erasure are technologically feasible and reasonably priced. This directly contradicts the original 'not required' clause, making the obligation ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup. However, if the Recipient has the technological ability to encrypt and completely erase them at a reasonable cost, they must do so. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Recipient is 'not required to destroy' backup files. The changed text imposes a *conditional requirement* to destroy them if encryption and erasure are technologically feasible and reasonably priced. This directly contradicts the original 'not required' clause in the contradicted text, making the obligation ambiguous. The change creates a direct and impactful contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 13. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, unless such information has been explicitly confirmed in writing by the Receiver’s Chief Financial Officer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text disclaims any representation or warranty regarding the accuracy of the confidential information. The added clause then introduces an exception where a written confirmation from the Receiver's CFO *does* create a representation or warranty, contradicting the general disclaimer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warrant y, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, unless such information has been explicitly confirmed in writing by the Receiver’s Chief Financial Officer. The Recipient agrees that neither the Receiver nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives relating to or resulting from use o f the Confidential Information by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives. The Recipient further understands and agrees that (i) the Receiver (a) shall be free to conduct the process f or a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such p rocess, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or enteri ng into a Definitive Agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each cas e, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall be free at its sole dis cretion to at any time accept or reject any
perturbation.justification: The original text states that there are no representations or warranties, express or implied, regarding the accuracy of the confidential information. The changed text introduces an exception, stating that a written confirmation from the Receiver's CFO does create a representation or warranty. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the addition of the exception creates a direct contradiction with the initial statement of no representations or warranties, as now under certain circumstances, there is. This creates an in-text contradiction, specifically misaligned terminology, because 'no representation or warranty' is now conditional.

perturbation.original_text: 15. In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Inf ormation, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. Further, the Recipient represents and warrants that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representative s have discussed or shared, and the Recipient hereby covenants that unless it has first received the written consent of the Receiver neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Re presentatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion, except in accordance with Section 4 or 8 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Information, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. However, the Recipient is permitted to engage in discussions with potential financing partners, without prior consent from the Receiver, provided that such partners also execute a confidentiality agreement with terms substantially similar to this one.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Recipient cannot discuss the Confidential Information with any third party without written consent. The added sentence then permits discussions with 'potential financing partners' under certain conditions, directly contradicting the 'no third party discussion' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Information, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. However, the Recipient is permitted to engage in discussions with potential financing partners, without prior consent from the Receiver, provided that such partners also execute a confidentiality agreement with terms substantially similar to this one. Further, the Recipient represents and warrants that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representative s have discussed or shared, and the Recipient hereby covenants that unless it has first received the written consent of the Receiver neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Re presentatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion, except in accordance with Section 4 or 8 of this Agreement. The Recipient acknowledges that th e effect of this covenant is that without the full disclosure to and the written consent of the R eceiver, neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representatives can act as agent, partn er, co-participant or co-venturer for any third party or third parties with respect to a proposed T ransaction. In order to obtain the consent of the Receiver, which the Receiver is entitled to withhol d in its sole discretion, the Recipient shall notif y the Receiver of the identity of each Person for who m or with whom the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives had considered pursuing a possible Transaction and the nature and interest the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Re presentatives and each such Person would have in respect of such possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits discussing confidential information with any third party without written consent. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing discussions with 'potential financing partners' under specific conditions, which directly contradicts the original blanket prohibition. The Contradicted Text also includes 'neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion'. This creates a clear contradiction.
